commit.message:
feat: wire 3 data pipeline gaps — upload taxonomy, API pull catalog, trigger auth

Gap 1 (upload taxonomy):
  - /api/ingest/upload now accepts taxonomy_node_id + taxonomy_node_type
  - Defaults to client-level scope when unspecified (taxonomy_node_type=client)
  - Validates uploader's taxonomy access (check_taxonomy_access) before accepting
  - Rejects with TAXONOMY_SCOPE_VIOLATION (HTTP 403) on access failure
  - /api/ingest/api_pull also accepts and persists taxonomy params
  - catalog_client.create_dataset() extended with optional taxonomy params
  - DataverseClient.check_taxonomy_access() helper added (queries mcmap_user_taxonomy_accesses)
  - ORC KB Data Protocol v9.0 extended with UPLOAD TAXONOMY ROUTING section

Gap 2 (API pull → catalog):
  - FL-084_ApiPullCatalog.json flow spec created for adapter → data substrate wiring
  - func-mcmap-adapters: CATALOG_CALLBACK_URL env var + async POST callback after each successful pull
  - Datasets inherit taxonomy scope from their API connection (mcmap_taxonomy_node_id/type)
  - Non-blocking: catalog callback failure logged as warning, adapter response unaffected

Gap 3 (trigger auth):
  - mcmap_trigger_registry schema v8.5.0: added taxonomy_node_id, taxonomy_node_type, created_by columns
  - FL-051 v8.5.0: re-validates creator's taxonomy access at execution time
  - Skips trigger + logs TRIGGER_AUTH_REVOKED if creator access revoked
  - revoked_triggers array added to FL-051 response for DVO monitoring
  - DVO_KB_Trigger_Management_v8.0.txt created: lifecycle, auth, orphan resolution, audit guidance

Authority: ACD-04-07, ACD-07-06, ACD-07-07, ACD-09-DA-02

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: 使用 tar.gz 取代 unzip（更通用）

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
via [Happy](https://happy.engineering)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Happy <yesreply@happy.engineering>

fix(deploy): stop recreating signal-desktop on push

Added --no-deps to prevent docker compose from recreating the desktop
container when rebuilding signal-ingest (which depends on it). Desktop
holds Signal link state and synced media — recreating it forces a
re-link and loses all 45 days of cached attachments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add mistake review phase after lesson practice

After the practice exercises, the app now checks Firestore for any
accumulated grammar mistakes. If found, it generates 3 AI exercises
(context-choice, error-correction, reverse-translation) targeting that
specific grammar point and mistake context, then enters a dedicated
"review" phase before the completion screen.

- New `lesson_mistakes` Firestore collection. Mistakes are upserted
  with a deterministic doc ID (uid + language + grammarFocus) so each
  grammar topic has at most one pending mistake per user.
- Wrong answers during practice automatically save a mistake (including
  a description of the exercise and correct answer for AI context).
- New `generateMistakeReview` server action calls Gemini to produce 3
  focused exercises for the failing grammar point.
- New `review` phase in lessonStore tracks index and correct count
  independently of the practice phase.
- If all 3 review exercises are answered correctly, the mistake document
  is deleted from Firestore. If the user has no mistakes, the review is
  skipped entirely.

commit.message:
feat: enforce Python 3.12+ minimum version in prereqs checker

prereqs.py previously only checked if a Python binary existed but not
its version. Add _parse_python_version() and _check_python_min_version()
to parse "Python X.Y.Z" strings and reject versions below 3.12.

When Python is found but too old, the tool reports found=false with a
"(need 3.12+)" annotation in the version string so users see what's wrong.

Closes: purser-7bs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: purser
repository.description: Unified agentic AI development framework combining Ralph Loop with Beads issue tracking

commit.message:
docs: Improve stacked PR workflow for collection branch and merge method (#5178)

* docs: Add collection branch safety warnings to PR workflow

Add explicit warnings to pr.mdc and create-java-pr skill that the
collection branch must never be manually merged or fast-forwarded.
Updating it causes GitHub to auto-merge all stack PRs and delete
their branches, destroying the entire stack.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: Include collection branch PR in stack list updates

The stack list update instructions only mentioned "all PRs in the
stack" which was ambiguous — it could be read as excluding the
collection branch PR. Now both pr.mdc and the create-java-pr skill
explicitly call out that the collection branch PR must also be updated
with the stack list.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: Add merge commit reminder to stack PR descriptions

Stack PRs must be merged with merge commits, not squash. Add a
reminder to the PR description so reviewers/mergers pick the right
method. This only applies to stack PRs — not standalone PRs or the
collection branch PR.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(r2): infinite retry with exponential backoff — uploads never fail

Upload retries indefinitely (1s, 2s, 4s, ... capped at 60s) until
success. R2 failures are always transient network issues — no reason
to give up and fall back to local storage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add python3→python→py fallback in prereqs checker

prereqs.py hardcoded `python3` as the only binary to check, which fails
on Windows (no python3) and in uv-managed environments (binary is `python`).

Add platform-aware fallback: on Unix try python3 then python; on Windows
try python then py -3. The reported tool name stays "python3" so
init.py's tool_map["python3"] continues to work unchanged.

Closes: purser-f3a

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix soundtrack playback, expand image coverage, optimize for testers

Soundtrack fix:
- AudioContext now explicitly unlocked on every user gesture type (click,
  touch, keydown, mousedown) via global listeners installed at init
- crossfadeTo() resumes AudioContext both after Howl creation and in load
  callback, catching edge cases where context is created after gesture
- This fixes the bug where soundtrack never played if user navigated via
  keyboard only (Howler's built-in unlock only listens for click/touch)

Image integration:
- Added 25+ new scene-to-image mappings using previously unused stills
  (Maja portrait, communal wailing, attestupa reactions, bear suit prep,
  Josh investigation, maypole dance, Pelle farewell)
- Fixed duplicate key errors in SCENE_IMAGE_MAP that caused TS failures
- Removed earlier duplicate entries, keeping better images from character
  moments section

Quality fixes:
- Fixed TS compilation errors (unused imports, duplicate object keys,
  invalid framer-motion transition prop on TransitionStinger)
- Updated index.html with proper title, description, OG meta tags,
  theme color, font preconnect, and inline SVG flower favicon
- TransitionStinger exit now properly uses slow 0.7s fade-out
- Added .serena/ to .gitignore

feat: stock-theme-app 전체 고도화 (Phase 1~5)

## Phase 1 — 안정성
- 종목코드 검증 스크립트 (scripts/validate_codes.py)
- themes.json 테마당 5종목 → 10종목 + name 필드 추가
- 네이버 API tenacity 재시도 로직 (3회, 지수 백오프)
- 알림 저장소 JSON → SQLite (aiosqlite, alert_history 테이블)

## Phase 2 — 시각화
- Dashboard 수평 막대 차트 (Recharts, 카드/차트 탭 전환)
- ThemeDetail 종목 등락률 바 차트 + 가격 범위 표시
- 테마 강도 히스토리 수집 (10분 주기) + API + 라인 차트

## Phase 3 — 기능 확장
- 종목 검색 API + SearchBar + StockModal 팝업
- 즐겨찾기 (localStorage, 탭 필터)
- 종목 상세 API (52주 최고/최저, PER, PBR, 시가총액)
- 알림 활성/비활성 토글 (PATCH API)
- 알림 이력 페이지 (GET /alerts/history)

## Phase 4 — UX/UI
- ThemeCard 스켈레톤 로딩 UI
- React ErrorBoundary
- Toast 알림 (생성/삭제 피드백)
- 모바일 반응형 (1열/2열/4열 그리드, 테이블 가로 스크롤)
- 다크모드 (CSS 변수 시스템, useTheme 훅, IndexBar 토글 버튼)

## Phase 5 — 배포
- nginx 내장 Docker 구조 (SPA 라우팅, API/WS 프록시)
- docker-compose.yml 재정비 (db_data 볼륨, healthcheck)
- GitHub Actions CI/CD (backend pytest, frontend build, docker build)

commit.message:
Build full SaaS dashboard with sidebar navigation

Complete dashboard redesign inspired by BabyLoveGrowth.ai:
- Dark theme sidebar with navigation (Overview, Content Plan, Analytics, Audit, Backlinks, Settings)
- Overview: welcome banner, LLM Visibility / Domain Rating / Health Score cards, potential reach by language, publish CTA
- Content Plan: article table with SEO scores, statuses (draft/ready/scheduled/published), keyword tracking
- Analytics: visitor stats, impressions, keyword positions, CTR, traffic chart
- Technical Audit: score circle, category breakdown (Performance, SEO, Accessibility, Content), issues list with severity
- Backlinks: empty state with CTA
- Settings: account info, site config, brand colors, danger zone
- Loads onboarding data from localStorage to personalize
- Mobile responsive with hamburger menu
- Auth protected via requireAuth()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: decouple translations from image loading to prevent timeout

The previous approach ran up to 4 sequential Gemini calls inside
fetchAllImageCache before returning any entries, easily exceeding
Vercel's 10s function timeout and causing no images to load.

- fetchAllImageCache now returns entries immediately (fast Firestore
  read only, no Gemini calls).
- A new translateMissingEntries action handles translations separately.
- The component fires translations as a parallel, non-blocking Promise
  after the images are already visible on screen.
- Added .catch() guards so neither step can crash the component.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Replace all <+trigger.payload.*> with <+pipeline.variables.*>

Add two new research essays on AI adoption

- The Six Percent: Why Most AI Investment Doesn't Return
  McKinsey 88/6 gap, Atlassian productivity paradox, what top performers
  actually do differently (dedicated ownership, structured enablement,
  workflow redesign). Data-driven, no fluff.

- The Mandate Trap: Why AI Mandates Fail Without Enablement
  Shopify vs. Duolingo comparison — same memo, opposite outcomes.
  Shopify had 3 years of infrastructure. Duolingo didn't. The mandate
  is the announcement after the strategy works, not the strategy itself.

Updated research index to list both essays at top.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(dashboard): use cumulative week budget for weekly progress bar

- Weekly progress bar now uses cumulative week budget (dailyBudget × weekNumber × 7)
- Weekly progress bar color uses weekly overrun status
- This makes weekly progress bar consistent with weekly text summary

Example (11 days, week 2, dailyBudget = 51.724):
- Daily progress: 671.500 / 568.965 = 118% (red, over)
- Weekly progress: 671.500 / 724.137 = 93% (primary, under)

commit.message:
GUI terminal: add full ELF support for spc and spe commands

The GUI terminal previously just printed a message saying spc/spe were
only available in the text console. This commit makes them work fully:

- Add syscall_set_gui_stdout() / syscall_set_gui_stdin() hooks to
  syscall.c so that sys_write(stdout/stderr) can be redirected to the
  GUI terminal buffer instead of UART during ELF execution.

- In terminal.c, spc and spe now call process_exec_args() to load and
  run the real /bin/spc and /bin/spe ELF binaries. Before launching,
  the I/O hooks are installed so all printf() output from the ELF
  appears in the terminal window. The hooks are cleared on return.

- The generic "command not found" fallback now checks VFS for
  /bin/<name> (or an absolute path) and runs it as an ELF if found,
  enabling any userspace binary to be launched from the GUI terminal.

- Update help text to reflect that spc/spe work directly in the GUI.

fix: CRITICAL — Remove wrong canonical tag that blocked Google indexing

The root layout set canonical: "/" which made EVERY page on the site
tell Google "I'm a duplicate of the homepage." This caused 0 pages
to be indexed for 3 months (confirmed by GSC coverage report).

Fix: Removed the global canonical from root layout metadata.

Also added www → non-www 301 redirect in middleware to consolidate
link equity and prevent duplicate content from www subdomain.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix OmniAuth CSRF session key mismatch (#138)

* Fix OmniAuth CSRF session key mismatch

OmniAuth's built-in AuthenticityTokenProtection uses Rack::Protection's
:csrf session key, but Rails stores its CSRF token under :_csrf_token.
This session key mismatch causes "Forbidden" errors on the admin login
form.

Disable OmniAuth's request_validation_phase since OAuth providers are
already protected by the state parameter in the callback phase.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix NavigationRegistry.reset! to re-register default user menu

reset! clears all class-level sections, including the "My Account"
bottom section registered during Configuration#initialize. When
nested_navigation_spec calls reset! in its after block, the user
menu disappears for any spec that runs later — causing
order-dependent failures in logins_spec (seed 30301).

Re-registering defaults after clearing ensures the user menu is
always present after a reset.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update reset! spec to expect default My Account section

The reset! method now re-registers the default user menu after
clearing, so the unit test must expect ["My Account"] instead
of an empty sections array.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Move H1 outside React root to fix SEO crawler detection

Issue: Bing still reports missing H1 tag despite previous commit.

Root cause:
- H1 was inside <div id="root"> which gets replaced by React
- Vite build process may strip content from inside root div
- Vercel was also serving stale cache from March 5th

Fix:
- Moved H1 BEFORE <div id="root"> (outside React mount point)
- H1 is visually hidden but accessible to crawlers
- Survives build process and React hydration
- Avoids double H1 (this is hidden, React renders visible H1)
- Now in static HTML that persists after build

Updated H1 text to match keyword optimization:
"Free Calorie Tracker - Track calories and macros with no paywalls or ads"

- Added prominent CTA boxes at TOP of each blog post

- CTAs emphasize relevant features (protein tracking, macro tracking, TDEE, etc.)

- 65 impressions on "how-to-count-calories" × 33% CTR = ~21 clicks/month
- 34 impressions on "how-to-calculate-tdee" × 33% CTR = ~11 clicks/month
- 24 impressions on "what-are-macros" × 33% CTR = ~8 clicks/month

commit.message:
Fix Dockerfile: simplify build stages and add .dockerignore

The previous Dockerfile had a broken COPY node_modules line and was
missing the public directory. Simplified to a clean two-stage build.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: adaptive sync sweep interval to reduce idle CPU

The sync sweep was running every 1s, calling BatchIterateSessions which
walks all ~16M hash buckets in the 10M-entry BPF session map — even when
there are zero sessions to sync. This caused __htab_map_lookup_and_delete_batch
to consume ~93% of bpfrxd CPU on idle cluster nodes.

Two optimizations:
1. Counter-based fast-path: read GLOBAL_CTR_SESSIONS_NEW/CLOSED (O(1)
   per-CPU counter reads) to skip the O(buckets) BatchIterate when no
   sessions have changed since the last empty sweep.
2. Adaptive interval: back off exponentially (1s → 2s → 4s → 8s → 10s)
   when nothing needs syncing. Return to 1s immediately when sessions
   are synced, keeping replication lag minimal under load.

Verified on loss cluster: bpfrxd CPU dropped from ~15% sustained to 0.0%.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: friendly startup progress in Docker entrypoint (#285)

* feat: add friendly startup progress messages to Docker entrypoint

Users running `docker run` now see clear step-by-step progress instead
of minimal log lines. Helps especially on first boot when init takes time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove misleading first-boot message, clean up completion banner

- Remove "First boot may take a few minutes" (shows on every startup)
- Use info() instead of ok() for banner separator lines

commit.message:
Replace special characters with random emojis

Toddlers don't need special characters like @#$%^& etc.
Keep letters, digits, space, and basic punctuation (. , ! ?).
Everything else gets swapped for a random emoji from a pool
of 150+ fun toddler-friendly emojis (animals, food, vehicles,
hearts, weather, sports, etc).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
